name: Ledge
description: Digital ruleset for the Ledge board game, expressed in a portable turn-based spec format.

state.description: Mapping of Ledge concepts into the generic G/data container.

dataFields.players: List of players with id, name, boardId, and elimination status.
dataFields.boards: Array of board states; each board owns 49 spaces with stacks, metadata, and adjacency.
dataFields.currentPlayerId: ID of the player whose turn it is.
dataFields.currentTurnPlacements: Placements made this turn (for enforcing Light+Dark).
dataFields.currentTurnMoves: Moves made this turn (for control semantics).
dataFields.winnerId: Optional winner player id when the game ends.
dataFields.gameOver: Boolean flag when no further moves are allowed.

phases.name: placement
phases.description: Each player must place exactly one Light and one Dark token per turn on their own board.

phases.name: movement
phases.description: Players move stacks across their own and other boards, leaving locks and resolving stacks/clears.

constraints: tone not already placed by current player this turn
effects: Add one token of given tone to the target stack.
When both Light and Dark have been placed, automatically advance to 'movement' phase.

constraints: source stack must be movable for given tone
destination must be adjacent (intra-board) or a valid cross-board ledge target
player must control the source space (own board or visited this turn)
effects: Remove one token of given tone from the source stack.
Apply Lock/Stack/Clear rules on destination stack.
Record move for control semantics and history.

constraints: in placement phase, may only end turn after both Light and Dark placed or if no valid placements remain
effects: Reset placement/move history for the new turn.
Set phase to 'placement' for the next player unless the game is over.

details.eliminationCondition: Player is eliminated when their center stack has a Dark token locked at the bottom (after resolving a move into their center).
details.winnerRule: If exactly one player remains non-eliminated, that player wins; if zero remain, the game ends with no winner.
